e95cd34.title: The Ledgers Remember
e95cd34.intro: After the Concord audit wiped the forged ledgers clean, we spent the week making sure the new ones actually stick. Your browser is no longer the bookkeeper — and this patch is the part where everything you earn, spend, pray, and heal remembers you when you log back in.
items: Combat experience — every swing, shot, and spell that lands XP is granted by the server and written to your save. Refresh the page and your Strength still knows what you did.
Prayer — toggling a prayer, draining points, recharging at an altar: all server-side now. Active prayers survive a reload; running dry still clears them, as it should.
Healing — the Aldgate fountain, Dr. Ticksworth's chair, and Glen's controversial cracker all heal through the server. That extra hitpoint is real chalk in the health ledger, not a browser fantasy.
Inventory — burying bones, eating food, and every other debit now targets the slot you actually used. No more phantom removals from the wrong pocket.
The Exchange, trading, and guild vaults — when the server rewrites your save mid-session, the client pulls the authoritative copy instead of clobbering it on the next autosave.
New adventurers — starter kit is defined by the server. Twenty-five coins in the bank, tools in the pack, glock on the hip: the same honest morning everyone gets.
e95cd34.signoff: The vale keeps its chalk. See you on the road. — The Larpscape team

aa9abc5.title: The Wardens' Second Pass
aa9abc5.intro: The Concord audit did not end when the ledgers were reset. This week we walked every road a forger might still take — and nailed shut the ones that were only pretending to be server-side. Your client can ask; the realm decides.
items: Death — you cannot declare yourself dead to shrug off a boss hit anymore. Only the server resolves when you fall, and only the server brings you back.
Movement — teleport claims are clamped. You cannot sprint across the map in a single heartbeat to mine, steal, or train from tiles you never reached.
Farming and snares — patch state, rake timing, and bird catches live in your save now, not in browser fiction. Harvest without planting earns nothing.
Agility courses — obstacle XP requires standing at the obstacle, with a honest cooldown between attempts. Frostpeak's summit lap finally reports to the right ledger.
Stations and shrines — cooking needs a range, smelting a furnace, smithing an anvil, prayer recharge an altar. Healing at fountains, chairs, and Glen requires you to actually be there.
The deep hazards — bog poison, lava edges, and Korr's eruption are server wounds now. Move off the telegraphed tile or eat the hit; the health bar will agree with the server.
Quest counters — Cold Comfort braziers, Hymn burials, Ravenmoor cellar bits, and Gullswreck crate searches are mark-for-mark server progress. Forged tallies no longer advance the story.
Sunscorch thieving — a failed pickpocket no longer stacks a second, client-only bruise on top of what the server already dealt.
aa9abc5.signoff: Thirty-four tricks tried the door. Thirty-four bounced. The wardens are satisfied — for now. — The Larpscape team
